Just sharing in case it helps someone.

metadata.content:
Here is something I found interesting about focus that most people don't know.

The more present you are and the more you focus on one thing at a time, the stronger you build your prefrontal cortex. That is the part of your brain often called the CEO. It is where you plan, solve problems, make decisions, and create.

Experienced meditators have a noticeably stronger prefrontal cortex, which is exactly why they have such a greater ability to handle whatever life throws at them.

So every time you sit down for a 90 minute focus block, remember this. You are not just getting more done. You are literally strengthening the part of your brain that keeps you regulated, clear, and in control when life gets chaotic.

That is the real ROI on focus.

Drop a comment below if you are doing focus blocks regularly. I want to hear how it is changing the way you operate. 👇
metadata.title: Why Focus is Building More Than Just Productivity

metadata.bio: Austin - Here to learn Wholesale!!

metadata.content:
Crazy little story from this week…
I was getting ready to head out to a local meetup when my phone lit up around 4:00 PM EST.
A wholesaler had a deal tied up on an RV park and needed a $10,000 earnest money deposit fast. Seller deadline was approaching, and the deal was on life support.
Instead of rushing blindly, we slowed down and did it right.
First, I needed to vet the lender — spoke with her directly, learned her funding structure, and made sure everything made sense.
Then I had the borrower introduce me to title so I could verify who was handling the file.
Spoke with title, reviewed the situation, and even discussed getting a mutual release signed if needed so everyone understood the process.
Next morning, I followed back up:
✔️ Confirmed documents were in order✔️ Verified wiring instructions✔️ Reconfirmed title was ready to receive funds
Then the wire was sent.
$10,000 EMD funded to title.
The best part?
We beat the seller’s deadline by 20 minutes.
Deals don’t always fall apart because they’re bad deals… sometimes they fall apart because people move too slow.
This one didn’t.
Real estate teaches you quickly: speed matters, but doing things the right way matters even more. If you need transactional funding let’s talk. Email: dlsfunds.denise@gmail.com
metadata.title: RV Park deal saved

metadata.bio: Through strong relationships we can help you fund your next opportunity, a purchase, rehab, refinancing, business funding or EMD.

metadata.content:
This absolute delight of a woman went out of her way to call me back after getting a handwritten note from me - just to tell me my handwriting looked “like a child wrote it” and that I abbreviated a town \(I’ve lived in for a decade\) incorrectly. At first, I was about to teach her a lesson on manners \(and maybe a little on safety too… haha jk… j…k… but really\). Then I calmed down because, honestly, I don’t have that kind of time. I realized okay, this person really needed me to know she prefers neat writing and official county abbreviations. Now I know. Hey, if that’s what gets me deals, I’ll do it!

Not-so-side note: she lives in the house, but she’s not the owner. Soooooo… a little probate contention prospect. I’ve moved on for now until the actual owner tells me to kick rocks.

Lesson learned: write neater because people notice, don’t abbreviate anymore, and most importantly: you don’t have time to get caught up dealing with people like that. Take the feedback and keep it moving.
metadata.title: Got Roasted for My Handwriting… Might Get a Deal Out of It

metadata.content:
What’s up guys? This post idea just came up to me in the moment, so I’ll just say it how it is as it comes.
Lately, I have been feeling like everything is stacked against me, cause I’m doing virtual wholesaling and don’t really have money to pay people to go look at a property for me. Heck, I honestly wouldn’t really trust people here to do that without trying to steal my shit, but I may in the future. It’s hard to describe this feeling. It’s like I know I can find a way around it. Heck, I even know that there may be owners that are really driven to sell and wouldn’t even care about an inspection. But I do know that there are ways around it. I could just have the cash buyer go, cause they are going to do it anyways. No one would buy a house without a walkthrough haha.

I guess what I really am asking here is what have you guys done? The ones in similar positions to me. How have you guys got a property locked up without going to see the property or boots on the ground? And yeah, that’s the real question, and perhaps I already answered it myself. How do you lock a property under contract if you don’t go and see the property yourself or have boots on the ground?
I guess my only option is just getting a buyer to go and do the walkthrough.

I feel like this is a dumb question due to how obvious the answer is, but at least I got to vent and bitch. If anything, I think I need to let go of this notion that motivated sellers won’t lock up a deal without someone coming to see the property first. Ah, that makes sense. I think I just self-diagnosed my little issue that was causing some anxiety. It’s not that I can’t have someone go and look at the property. That’s not my real issue. What's really going on with me is that I’ve had this underlying fear that the owners of these properties would in no way, shape, or form ever sign a contract with me to purchase their home without me or anyone else seeing it. So I just got to focus on the sellers that don't give a fuck and let me lock it up without stepping foot inside. I'll just tell them my partner\(buyer\) will eventually do it.
metadata.title: Virtual Wholesaling | No One Has To See The Property To Lock It Up

metadata.content:
Hi all. A lot of property business owners I've spoken to have tested automation and felt like it didn't move the needle.

It's almost never the tool. It's where they pointed it.

Starting with back-end stuff like maintenance tracking, landlord reports and admin makes operations slightly cheaper. But it doesn't grow their business.

The front end is where property businesses actually scale. Here's the framework, RACE:

Reach — get in front of more landlords, vendors, or tenants
Acquire — respond to enquiries fast enough to actually win the lead
Close — follow up consistently until they book or sign
Expand — keep existing landlords happy so they stay and refer others

Slow response to an enquiry? Acquire problem. Losing landlords quietly? Expand problem. Both fixable.

Hope this helps 🙏
metadata.title: Why Automation Isn't Growing Your Property Business (And Where to Point It Instead)

metadata.bio: My name is Michael Fennimore. I work in the FL and KY markets.

metadata.content:
Made 155 calls tonight!
Had 5 inbound calls! -All not interested.
24 pick ups
11 not interested.
7 wrong numbers.
5 hang ups
1 interested in selling ASAP!
Was fighting hard today. First time I started to crash out while calling!! Got a few too many no answers in a row and Oh man! Had to snap out of that one. Get up n go take a breather. But once I got back at it that's when I connected with that homeowner wanting to sell now! They weren't as open to talking about the property as others have been and was rather quick to get off the phone when he heard I didn't have an offer for him right then. I'm but I will call him back tomorrow and present a few offers and see what happens!
After doing some reflecting.. Im seeing I need to be making multiple offers a day and I'm just not. That is one thing that NEEDS to change everyday. I was doing it but things just slowed down after hitting a bunch of voicemails for my follow ups day after day. I still have quite a few offers that haven't been presented just because I haven't been able to get them on the phone again. All I can do is keep trying everyday. Until then! I will keep building.
Excited to see how good tomorrow can really get!!
1 New Lead Added to the pipeline!!!

YOU CAN'T STOP ME!!!!
ITS TIME TO LEVEL UP!!!!!!
LETTSSS GOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!!!

metadata.bio:
Founder @XLeads.com 🚀 Helping Wholesalers Close their First Deal w/ My Skool Group!
250k+ YouTube Subscribers | 26 y/o 8 Figure Wholesaler

metadata.content:
Welcome to the largest wholesaling community in existence! Read your Getting Started Guide below and let’s get you to your first deal — fast.

➡️ This isn’t just another free group — this is a $7,000 community that I’m giving you access to for FREE.
I’ve turned down 8-figure coaching offers to make this available to action-takers like you — so treat it like it’s worth something… because it is. \(Or I might just start charging 😅\)

➡️ We’re a Family.
Drop an intro below — tell us where you’re from and why you joined. We want to know you, support you, and celebrate your wins.

➡️ Start with the “Classroom” section.
That’s where you’ll find my full wholesaling course — packed with step-by-step instructions, in-depth training, and every downloadable tool you need. Everything you’re wondering about? It’s probably answered in there.

➡️ Check the Calendar and lock in your weekly calls.
We go live 5x a week with free coaching and Q&A — don’t miss a session. Go to the Calendar section now and set reminders.

➡️ Engage to Level Up.
Comment. Post. Contribute. As you level up, you unlock bonus content, the ability to post, DM, and access extra courses. The more you give, the more you get.

➡️ Most importantly: TAKE MASSIVE ACTION.
Don’t wait. Call a seller. Send a text. Knock a door. Take action TODAY.
Your first deal starts with one move — and we’re here to help you close it.

Let’s get it 💪
Welcome to the community.

metadata.content:
I’ve been taking the time to write notes and mail them out. I sent 13 letters to highly distressed sellers from a government list and got a call back. She said she’s “staying for now,” but realistically she can’t. Now I have her direct line so I can follow up through mail or call/text.

My first deal came from postcards, but that was more of a spray and pray approach. Now I’m working off a step 1-5 process every day, like I’m at work. Last week, that led to four real conversations!

Just sharing in case it helps someone.

metadata.title: What markets are you wholesaling in right now?

metadata.bio: My buddy has a nationwide cash buyer network. Do you need help with Dispo?

metadata.content:
Here is something I found interesting about focus that most people don't know.

The more present you are and the more you focus on one thing at a time, the stronger you build your prefrontal cortex. That is the part of your brain often called the CEO. It is where you plan, solve problems, make decisions, and create.

Experienced meditators have a noticeably stronger prefrontal cortex, which is exactly why they have such a greater ability to handle whatever life throws at them.

So every time you sit down for a 90 minute focus block, remember this. You are not just getting more done. You are literally strengthening the part of your brain that keeps you regulated, clear, and in control when life gets chaotic.

That is the real ROI on focus.

Drop a comment below if you are doing focus blocks regularly. I want to hear how it is changing the way you operate. 👇
metadata.title: Why Focus is Building More Than Just Productivity

metadata.content:
Crazy little story from this week…
I was getting ready to head out to a local meetup when my phone lit up around 4:00 PM EST.
A wholesaler had a deal tied up on an RV park and needed a $10,000 earnest money deposit fast. Seller deadline was approaching, and the deal was on life support.
Instead of rushing blindly, we slowed down and did it right.
First, I needed to vet the lender — spoke with her directly, learned her funding structure, and made sure everything made sense.
Then I had the borrower introduce me to title so I could verify who was handling the file.
Spoke with title, reviewed the situation, and even discussed getting a mutual release signed if needed so everyone understood the process.
Next morning, I followed back up:
✔️ Confirmed documents were in order✔️ Verified wiring instructions✔️ Reconfirmed title was ready to receive funds
Then the wire was sent.
$10,000 EMD funded to title.
The best part?
We beat the seller’s deadline by 20 minutes.
Deals don’t always fall apart because they’re bad deals… sometimes they fall apart because people move too slow.
This one didn’t.
Real estate teaches you quickly: speed matters, but doing things the right way matters even more. If you need transactional funding let’s talk. Email: dlsfunds.denise@gmail.com
metadata.title: RV Park deal saved

metadata.bio: Through strong relationships we can help you fund your next opportunity, a purchase, rehab, refinancing, business funding or EMD.

metadata.content:
This absolute delight of a woman went out of her way to call me back after getting a handwritten note from me - just to tell me my handwriting looked “like a child wrote it” and that I abbreviated a town \(I’ve lived in for a decade\) incorrectly. At first, I was about to teach her a lesson on manners \(and maybe a little on safety too… haha jk… j…k… but really\). Then I calmed down because, honestly, I don’t have that kind of time. I realized okay, this person really needed me to know she prefers neat writing and official county abbreviations. Now I know. Hey, if that’s what gets me deals, I’ll do it!

Not-so-side note: she lives in the house, but she’s not the owner. Soooooo… a little probate contention prospect. I’ve moved on for now until the actual owner tells me to kick rocks.

Lesson learned: write neater because people notice, don’t abbreviate anymore, and most importantly: you don’t have time to get caught up dealing with people like that. Take the feedback and keep it moving.
metadata.title: Got Roasted for My Handwriting… Might Get a Deal Out of It

metadata.content:
What’s up guys? This post idea just came up to me in the moment, so I’ll just say it how it is as it comes.
Lately, I have been feeling like everything is stacked against me, cause I’m doing virtual wholesaling and don’t really have money to pay people to go look at a property for me. Heck, I honestly wouldn’t really trust people here to do that without trying to steal my shit, but I may in the future. It’s hard to describe this feeling. It’s like I know I can find a way around it. Heck, I even know that there may be owners that are really driven to sell and wouldn’t even care about an inspection. But I do know that there are ways around it. I could just have the cash buyer go, cause they are going to do it anyways. No one would buy a house without a walkthrough haha.

I guess what I really am asking here is what have you guys done? The ones in similar positions to me. How have you guys got a property locked up without going to see the property or boots on the ground? And yeah, that’s the real question, and perhaps I already answered it myself. How do you lock a property under contract if you don’t go and see the property yourself or have boots on the ground?
I guess my only option is just getting a buyer to go and do the walkthrough.

I feel like this is a dumb question due to how obvious the answer is, but at least I got to vent and bitch. If anything, I think I need to let go of this notion that motivated sellers won’t lock up a deal without someone coming to see the property first. Ah, that makes sense. I think I just self-diagnosed my little issue that was causing some anxiety. It’s not that I can’t have someone go and look at the property. That’s not my real issue. What's really going on with me is that I’ve had this underlying fear that the owners of these properties would in no way, shape, or form ever sign a contract with me to purchase their home without me or anyone else seeing it. So I just got to focus on the sellers that don't give a fuck and let me lock it up without stepping foot inside. I'll just tell them my partner\(buyer\) will eventually do it.
metadata.title: Virtual Wholesaling | No One Has To See The Property To Lock It Up

metadata.content:
Hi all. A lot of property business owners I've spoken to have tested automation and felt like it didn't move the needle.

It's almost never the tool. It's where they pointed it.

Starting with back-end stuff like maintenance tracking, landlord reports and admin makes operations slightly cheaper. But it doesn't grow their business.

The front end is where property businesses actually scale. Here's the framework, RACE:

Reach — get in front of more landlords, vendors, or tenants
Acquire — respond to enquiries fast enough to actually win the lead
Close — follow up consistently until they book or sign
Expand — keep existing landlords happy so they stay and refer others

Slow response to an enquiry? Acquire problem. Losing landlords quietly? Expand problem. Both fixable.

Hope this helps 🙏
metadata.title: Why Automation Isn't Growing Your Property Business (And Where to Point It Instead)

metadata.bio: My name is Michael Fennimore. I work in the FL and KY markets.

metadata.content:
Made 155 calls tonight!
Had 5 inbound calls! -All not interested.
24 pick ups
11 not interested.
7 wrong numbers.
5 hang ups
1 interested in selling ASAP!
Was fighting hard today. First time I started to crash out while calling!! Got a few too many no answers in a row and Oh man! Had to snap out of that one. Get up n go take a breather. But once I got back at it that's when I connected with that homeowner wanting to sell now! They weren't as open to talking about the property as others have been and was rather quick to get off the phone when he heard I didn't have an offer for him right then. I'm but I will call him back tomorrow and present a few offers and see what happens!
After doing some reflecting.. Im seeing I need to be making multiple offers a day and I'm just not. That is one thing that NEEDS to change everyday. I was doing it but things just slowed down after hitting a bunch of voicemails for my follow ups day after day. I still have quite a few offers that haven't been presented just because I haven't been able to get them on the phone again. All I can do is keep trying everyday. Until then! I will keep building.
Excited to see how good tomorrow can really get!!
1 New Lead Added to the pipeline!!!

YOU CAN'T STOP ME!!!!
ITS TIME TO LEVEL UP!!!!!!
LETTSSS GOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!!!
